topic: A person opening a bottle of Coca-Cola with the new attached tethered cap, side view.

parsed_generation.script:
Shot 1: Side view of a person holding a cold Coca-Cola bottle. Condensation beads on the bottle as the tethered cap sits neatly at the neck, clearly visible in the frame. The scene feels refreshing and ready for a moment on the go.
Shot 2: Close-up on the cap and tether. A slim cord connects the cap to the bottle, highlighting the new tethered design.
Shot 3: The person grips the cap and begins to twist to open. The cap detaches from the bottle but remains attached to the tether, so it never fully disappears from view.
Shot 4: The cap rests at the end of its tether as the bottle tilts to pour, showing the opening while the tether stays connected.
Shot 5: A splash of Coca-Cola pours into a glass; bubbles rise and fizz emphasis the refreshing moment.
Shot 6: On-screen text appears: Less mess. Less litter. More moments.
Shot 7: Brand close-out with Coca-Cola logo and a simple tagline on screen: Coca-Cola. Refreshment in every moment.
parsed_generation.narration: Introducing Coca-Cola with the new tethered cap. From this side view, you can see the cap attached to the bottle by a slim tether. Twist to open, and the cap detaches, yet remains tethered so you never lose it. Pour a cold sip, enjoy the fizz, and experience the convenience of a cap that stays with you. Coca-Cola — refreshment that sticks with you.

feedback: The output stays on the person and bottle with the tethered cap and follows a clear sequence, but could tighten the pacing. Consider reducing redundancy and ensuring every shot reinforces the tethered-cap concept with fewer on-screen texts. The narration should add unique context that isn't obvious from visuals alone, such as benefits or usage scenarios.
strengths: Clear, shot-by-shot progression of the tethered cap design.
Concise branding moment tied to the product feature.
fixes: Minimize shot count to 4-5 if possible to maintain focus on the open-and-pour action.
Trim narration to emphasize the tether mechanic without restating what is visually evident.
